details.explanation: She reliably extracts the important details from written agreements, invoices, and official notices. details.explanation: She weighs evidence instead of accepting explanations at face value. details.explanation: She can work through unfamiliar problems by breaking them into steps: verify, compare, document, escalate. details.explanation: She does not accept work as complete until it matches the agreement. She reviews invoices, keeps records, and withholds approval when details do not line up. This makes her very hard to fool, but also hard to satisfy if documentation is sloppy.

details.explanation: She does not bargain emotionally or concede under pressure. When terms change mid-job, she stops the work, seeks a second opinion, and uses documentation to hold her ground. Her style is effective for self-protection, but it is more hardline than collaborative.
